headlines.title: Nikkei 225 on edge as Japanese yen resumes its slide, but a bullish pattern emerges
Japan’s Topix Rises on Lower Oil Prices, Nikkei Stock Average Weighed down by Tech
Asian shares rise as tech rebound holds, oil slips
A line in the sand for the world’s largest creditor has been breached
Daily Brief Thematic (Sector/Industry): Ohayo Japan | US Stocks Higher as Weak Jobs Data Dims Rate Hike Expectations and more
Japan's Topix rises on lower oil prices, Nikkei weighed down by tech
Japanese Stocks Climb as Oil Slides on Easing Middle East Tensions

marketTone.ko: 오늘 일본 증시는 혼조세를 보였습니다. 토픽스 지수는 유가 하락과 중동 긴장 완화에 힘입어 상승했으며, 이는 아시아 전반의 기술주 반등에도 기여했습니다. 그러나 닛케이 225 지수는 일부 기술주의 부진과 엔화 약세 지속으로 경계심을 유지했으며, 특히 엔화 가치 하락은 세계 최대 채권국인 일본에 중요한 선을 넘어선 것으로 평가됩니다. 한편, 미국 고용 지표 둔화로 인한 금리 인상 기대감 약화는 글로벌 증시에 긍정적인 영향을 미쳤습니다.
marketTone.en: Japanese stocks presented a mixed picture today. The Topix index gained, largely supported by lower oil prices and easing Middle East tensions, which also fueled a broader Asian tech rebound. However, the Nikkei 225 remained cautious, weighed down by specific tech sector performance and the yen's resumed slide, a development noted as breaching a significant threshold for Japan. Global market sentiment was positively influenced by weak US jobs data, which tempered rate hike expectations.
